column1: This is a template for the testcases csv file. [PLACEHODERS] need to be replaced with the final value.
The rest of the template contains sample data that needs to be tailored to the actual specification.
Author-oriented test cases. All steps can be performed by a content author in AEM Editor or on the published site.

column5: Step Action
column6: Step Expected

column3: Layout Container | Author can find and use Responsive Layout Gap in Style System

column5: Open a page in AEM Editor that contains a Layout Container (or add one)
column6: Page is open in edit mode with a Layout Container

column5: Select the Layout Container and open the Style System (e.g. from component toolbar or properties)
column6: Style System dialog opens

column5: Look for a style group about gap or spacing between components
column6: A style group named 'Responsive Layout Gap' (or equivalent) is visible

column5: Check the options under that group
column6: Exactly three options are available: Small, Medium, and Large

column5: Select one option (e.g. Medium) and apply; then try selecting another
column6: Only one option can be active at a time; applying a different option replaces the previous choice

column5: Close the dialog and look at the page preview
column6: Page preview updates; no errors appear

column3: Layout Container | Selecting a gap style shows visible space between components on the same row

column5: Open or create a page with a Layout Container that has two components side by side (e.g. two components each using 6 columns)
column6: Page shows two components next to each other in one row

column5: Open Style System for that Layout Container and select Responsive Layout Gap 'Medium'
column6: Style is applied

column5: View the page (in preview or on the published site)
column6: A visible horizontal gap appears between the two components

column5: Change the style to 'Small' and view again
column6: The gap is smaller than with Medium

column5: Change the style to 'Large' and view again
column6: The gap is larger than with Medium

column3: Layout Container | Gap appears only between components, not at the outer edges

column5: Use a Layout Container with two components side by side and Responsive Layout Gap (e.g. Medium) applied
column6: Layout with gap style is ready

column5: View the page and look at the left edge of the first component
column6: There is no extra empty space before the first component

column5: Look at the right edge of the last component on the same row
column6: There is no extra empty space after the last component

column5: Look at the space between the two components
column6: The gap appears only between the two components

column3: Layout Container | On narrow screens, components stack without extra vertical spacing

column5: Use a Layout Container with two components side by side and Responsive Layout Gap applied
column6: Layout with gap is ready

column5: View the page at desktop width
column6: A horizontal gap is visible between the two components

column5: Resize the browser to a narrow width (e.g. mobile) so the two components stack vertically
column6: Components stack into two full-width rows

column5: Look at the vertical space between the two stacked components
column6: Vertical spacing looks normal; there is no obvious double spacing or extra gap between the stacked components

column3: Layout Container | When no gap style is selected, components touch as before

column5: Open a page with a Layout Container that has two components side by side
column6: Layout with two components in one row is ready

column5: Ensure no Responsive Layout Gap style is selected (open Style System and leave Responsive Layout Gap unset if needed)
column6: No gap style is applied

column5: View the page
column6: The two components sit next to each other with no visible gap between them

column3: Layout Container | Single component or full-width row: gap style has no visible effect

column5: Add or open a Layout Container that has only one component (e.g. one component using full width)
column6: Container with a single component is ready

column5: Open Style System and select Responsive Layout Gap (e.g. Medium)
column6: Style is applied

column5: View the page
column6: The layout looks normal; no odd empty space or broken layout appears

column3: Layout Container | Gap style works together with other container styles

column5: Open a Layout Container that has two components side by side
column6: Layout with two components is ready

column5: In Style System, apply another style you normally use (e.g. a background colour, or a spacing style)
column6: That style is applied

column5: In Style System, also select Responsive Layout Gap (e.g. Medium)
column6: Both styles are applied

column5: View the page
column6: The other style (e.g. background or spacing) still looks correct, and a gap is visible between the two components

column3: Layout Container | Gap size looks consistent at different screen sizes

column5: Use a Layout Container with two components side by side and Responsive Layout Gap (e.g. Medium) applied
column6: Layout with gap is ready

column5: View the page at desktop width and note how the gap looks
column6: Gap is visible

column5: Resize the browser to tablet width (so the two components are still side by side)
column6: Layout remains side by side

column5: Compare the gap between the components
column6: The gap looks the same as at desktop; it does not suddenly get much bigger or smaller

column3: Layout Container | Published page shows the gap correctly

column5: On a page with a Layout Container (two components side by side), apply Responsive Layout Gap (e.g. Medium)
column6: Gap style is applied in the editor

column5: Publish the page
column6: Page is published successfully

column5: Open the published page in a browser (outside the editor)
column6: Published page loads

column5: Check the area where the two components sit side by side
column6: The same gap is visible on the published page

column3: Layout Container | Existing pages without the gap style look unchanged

column5: Open an existing page that has Layout Containers with components side by side and where you have not applied Responsive Layout Gap
column6: Existing page is open

column5: View the page (preview or published)
column6: The layout looks the same as before; components that were touching are still touching; no unexpected spacing or layout change appears
